list.name: Changes
list.comment:
Add parsePrice for FIX price fields (Step 2)
Parse a byte-range FIX price to long cents, reusing parseLong for the integer and fractional parts. Scale the fraction by digit length, not value: one digit *10, two digits as-is.
Reject (-1L sentinel, consistent with parseLong) on: empty range, empty integer part, more than two decimals, non-digit bytes, and non-positive result. The zero/negative price policy is applied here via the > 0 check, since parseLong deliberately treats "0" as a valid parse.
PRICE_SCALE and MAX_DECIMALS kept private to the parser (no second caller). No String/split/BigDecimal; pure byte[] in, off the hot path.
Tested: integer-only, one/two decimals, leading zeros, zero-integer-part with positive fraction, and rejects for >2 decimals, zero, negative, bare dot, empty integer part, trailing dot, trailing junk, and multiple dots. Tests embed each price in a wider buffer to verify offset/length bounds.

task.summary: Initial commit: Order Book Demo project
Added trade record and fill method to Order class. Currently implementing the matching engine logic.
Added trade record and fill method to Order class. Currently implementing the matching engine logic.
Implementing logic for matching buy orders with best ask orders
Implemented matching for buy and sell orders
Implemented matching for buy and sell orders

feat: implement order submission, cancellation, and book display
- addOrder: routes to matchBuy/matchSell, rests unfilled quantity - addToBook: inserts order into price level queue via computeIfAbsent - cancelOrder: marks order cancelled and removes from book with O(n) lookup - printBook: displays current bid/ask book state

feat: add IdGenerator for order, trade, and participant IDs
- Static utility class with AtomicLong counters - Replaces hardcoded 0L trade IDs in matchBuy/matchSell
Did some file restructuring to organize classes
Did some file restructuring to organize classes

Implemented step 0 of the order gateway: added FixConstants holding SOH = 0x01, EQUALS = '='. tag numbers: 8, 9, 10, 35, 11, 38, 41, 44, 54, 55. msg-type bytes: 'D', 'F'. the single configured instrument symbol for the book

Implemented step 1 of the order gateway:parseLong(byte[] buf, int start, int end). Goal: pure byte-range to long. Foundation for every numeric parse downstream. Walk bytes [start, end). For each: digit = b - '0', reject if outside 0..9, accumulate val = val * 10 + digit.As well as some unit tests

Add parsePrice for FIX price fields (Step 2)
Parse a byte-range FIX price to long cents, reusing parseLong for the integer and fractional parts. Scale the fraction by digit length, not value: one digit *10, two digits as-is.
Reject (-1L sentinel, consistent with parseLong) on: empty range, empty integer part, more than two decimals, non-digit bytes, and non-positive result. The zero/negative price policy is applied here via the > 0 check, since parseLong deliberately treats "0" as a valid parse.
PRICE_SCALE and MAX_DECIMALS kept private to the parser (no second caller). No String/split/BigDecimal; pure byte[] in, off the hot path.
Tested: integer-only, one/two decimals, leading zeros, zero-integer-part with positive fraction, and rejects for >2 decimals, zero, negative, bare dot, empty integer part, trailing dot, trailing junk, and multiple dots. Tests embed each price in a wider buffer to verify offset/length bounds.

MESSAGE.value:
Initial commit: Order Book Demo project
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Initial commit: Order Book Demo project
Added trade record and fill method to Order class. Currently implementing the matching engine logic.
Implementing logic for matching buy orders with best ask orders
Implemented matching for buy and sell orders

feat: implement order submission, cancellation, and book display
- addOrder: routes to matchBuy/matchSell, rests unfilled quantity - addToBook: inserts order into price level queue via computeIfAbsent - cancelOrder: marks order cancelled and removes from book with O(n) lookup - printBook: displays current bid/ask book state

feat: add IdGenerator for order, trade, and participant IDs
- Static utility class with AtomicLong counters - Replaces hardcoded 0L trade IDs in matchBuy/matchSell
Did some file restructuring to organize classes

Implemented step 0 of the order gateway: added FixConstants holding SOH = 0x01, EQUALS = '='. tag numbers: 8, 9, 10, 35, 11, 38, 41, 44, 54, 55. msg-type bytes: 'D', 'F'. the single configured instrument symbol for the book

Implemented step 1 of the order gateway:parseLong(byte[] buf, int start, int end). Goal: pure byte-range to long. Foundation for every numeric parse downstream. Walk bytes [start, end). For each: digit = b - '0', reject if outside 0..9, accumulate val = val * 10 + digit.As well as some unit tests

Add parsePrice for FIX price fields (Step 2)
Parse a byte-range FIX price to long cents, reusing parseLong for the integer and fractional parts. Scale the fraction by digit length, not value: one digit *10, two digits as-is.
Reject (-1L sentinel, consistent with parseLong) on: empty range, empty integer part, more than two decimals, non-digit bytes, and non-positive result. The zero/negative price policy is applied here via the > 0 check, since parseLong deliberately treats "0" as a valid parse.
PRICE_SCALE and MAX_DECIMALS kept private to the parser (no second caller). No String/split/BigDecimal; pure byte[] in, off the hot path.
Tested: integer-only, one/two decimals, leading zeros, zero-integer-part with positive fraction, and rejects for >2 decimals, zero, negative, bare dot, empty integer part, trailing dot, trailing junk, and multiple dots. Tests embed each price in a wider buffer to verify offset/length bounds.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value:
Add parsePrice for FIX price fields (Step 2)
Parse a byte-range FIX price to long cents, reusing parseLong for the integer and fractional parts. Scale the fraction by digit length, not value: one digit *10, two digits as-is.
Reject (-1L sentinel, consistent with parseLong) on: empty range, empty integer part, more than two decimals, non-digit bytes, and non-positive result. The zero/negative price policy is applied here via the > 0 check, since parseLong deliberately treats "0" as a valid parse.
PRICE_SCALE and MAX_DECIMALS kept private to the parser (no second caller). No String/split/BigDecimal; pure byte[] in, off the hot path.
Tested: integer-only, one/two decimals, leading zeros, zero-integer-part with positive fraction, and rejects for >2 decimals, zero, negative, bare dot, empty integer part, trailing dot, trailing junk, and multiple dots. Tests embed each price in a wider buffer to verify offset/length bounds.
